examples.title: Event Handling with ActionListener
examples.description: Demonstrates Swing's event-driven architecture using button click handling
examples.explanation:
1. Create JFrame and JButton
2. Add ActionListener interface implementation
3. Override actionPerformed() for event handling
4. Show dialog on button click

examples.title: MVC Pattern Implementation
examples.description: Basic MVC structure with separate Model, View, and Controller components
examples.explanation:
1. Model manages data (counter value)
2. View handles UI presentation
3. Controller mediates interactions
4. Loose coupling between components

examples.title: Swing Event Handling Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM EventHandlingProcess
BEGIN
 1. User interacts with component (e.g., clicks button)
 2. Component generates Event object
 3. Event dispatched to registered Listeners
 4. Listener's event handler method executes (e.g., actionPerformed)
 5. Application state updates based on event
END
examples.explanation:
1. Event source identification
2. Event object encapsulation
3. Listener notification mechanism
4. Handler method execution

examples.title: MVC Workflow Algorithm
examples.description: Sequence of operations in Model-View-Controller pattern
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MVCWorkflow
BEGIN
 1. User interacts with View component
 2. View notifies Controller
 3. Controller updates Model state
 4. Model notifies View of changes
 5. View refreshes display
END
examples.explanation:
1. Separation of concerns
2. Change propagation mechanism
3. Observer pattern implementation
4. Decoupled components interaction

examples.title: Pluggable Look & Feel Demo
examples.explanation:
1. UIManager controls global L&F
2. Cross-platform vs system-specific styles
3. Runtime appearance changes
4. Consistent theming across components
